news.title: Punjab & Haryana HC bars fresh allotments in Oberoi Realty’s Gurugram project pending DTCP decision
news.para: The Punjab & Haryana High Court has restrained IREO Group entities and Oberoi Realty from making fresh allotments or creating third-party rights in a Gurugram project until the Haryana DTCP decides a complaint by Advance India Projects. Oberoi Realty said the order does not affect existing sales or construction.

news.title: Merger of banks does not shield tenant from eviction under Delhi Rent Control Act: SC
news.para: The Supreme Court ruled bank mergers do not exempt tenants from eviction without landlord consent.  Punjab National Bank must vacate premises by January 2027 after a long legal battle.  The court found amalgamation did not override the Delhi Rent Control Act's provisions.  This decision ends a dispute dating back to 1987.  The ruling clarifies tenant responsibilities in bank mergers.

news.title: Gurugram realty fraud: Delhi court says ED summons to foreign-based son of accused invalid
news.para: A Delhi court ruled ED summons invalid for a foreign national residing abroad.  The agency must follow legal procedures for serving overseas individuals.  This decision impacts the Enforcement Directorate's investigation into real estate fraud allegations.  Amit Katyal, the accused, sought to restrain the ED from valid service on his son.  The court emphasized due process for individuals living outside India.

news.title: Amaravati phase-I work on track, to be completed by next year: Andhra Pradesh minister
news.para: Minister P Narayana confirmed Amaravati construction proceeds according to schedule.  He dismissed opposition claims and misleading comparisons regarding project costs.  The first phase of the capital project is expected to finish by 2027.  Key infrastructure like MLA quarters are completed and others near completion.  The government aims for efficient governance and improved public services in Amaravati.

news.title: Chennai development body conducts stakeholder consultations for third master plan
news.para: Greater Chennai Corporation proposed climate-resilient planning and waterbody protection for Chennai's future. GIS-based monitoring and stronger social infrastructure were also key suggestions made. The master plan aims to guide Chennai's growth until the year 2046. Discussions included accommodating higher densities in core areas or added regions. Valid suggestions will be included in the master plan for government presentation.

news.title: After ‘no new tax’ budget, Nagpur civic body plans property tax hike
news.para: A proposal to increase property tax by ten percent has been placed on the agenda. This hike is expected to generate an additional fifteen point forty-two crore rupees for the NMC. The proposal also seeks to increase sewerage and fire benefit taxes from one to two percent. Overall, property tax collections are estimated to see an average ten percent increase.

news.title: Amazon Seller Services leases over 4.17 lakh sq ft warehouse in Bhiwandi near Mumbai
news.para: Amazon India has taken a warehousing facility in Bhiwandi through a long-term lease, set for nine years beginning June 2026. The financial commitment involves a monthly rent exceeding Rs 1.27 crore. This strategic move highlights Amazon's commitment to enhancing its logistics infrastructure and fortifying its supply chain.

news.title: DPCC rolls out Dust Portal 2.0 to tighten monitoring of construction-related pollution in Delhi
news.para: Delhi construction projects must now submit compliance reports and install air quality sensors.  The upgraded Dust Portal 2.0 requires live camera feeds for real-time pollution monitoring.  Projects over 500 square metres must register and map site boundaries using GIS technology.  Self-audit reports covering 12 parameters will be submitted every 15 days.  This initiative aims to strengthen oversight and reduce dust emissions from construction activities.

news.title: OC amnesty scheme for Mumbai buildings occupied before 2016 approved
news.para: Mumbai's BMC standing committee approved an amnesty scheme for occupation certificates. This scheme grants certificates to buildings occupied without them for years. Families residing in homes up to 80 square meters will receive urgent relief. The proposal aims to address issues faced by citizens due to delayed OCs. This move provides a pathway for legalizing homes occupied before November 2016.

news.title: NGT seeks response from CAQM, others on builder's plea against closure notice
news.para: The National Green Tribunal is reviewing a Haryana construction company's appeal.  This appeal challenges a closure notice issued by the Commission for Air Quality Management.  The company previously challenged this notice before the Punjab and Haryana High Court.  That court dismissed the challenge as withdrawn after granting interim protection.  The tribunal has now issued notices to the relevant authorities for their responses.

news.title: Delhi development body invites builder entities for TOD policy
news.para: The Delhi Development Authority has begun inviting developer entities for planned development. This policy focuses on areas near metro and rapid rail corridors for mixed land use. Developers will apply through a single online system for all necessary approvals. The initiative aims to enhance affordable housing and improve metro ridership. This planned development will create new housing and related infrastructure.

news.title: UK builder Vistry expects CEO-led overhaul to deliver recovery after first-half loss
news.para: Britain's Vistry anticipates a first-half pre-tax loss of thirty million pounds.  Cost cuts and reduced land purchases will drive second-half profitability.  The company plans to decrease its regional footprint and cease part exchange operations.  Annual profit targets remain unchanged despite current economic challenges.  Vistry expects to end the year with net cash exceeding one hundred million pounds.

news.title: Jashn Golf Estate champions low-density luxury: Why Jashn Realty is betting on a 48-tower low-rise community over the high-rise luxury model
news.para: As urban homebuyers increasingly seek privacy, open spaces and wellness-driven lifestyles, Jashn Golf Estate introduces a new philosophy of luxury living that prioritises exclusivity over vertical density.

news.title: Delhi CM orders check on stamps duty evasion in property registrations through GPAs
news.para: Delhi Chief Minister Rekha Gupta has issued strict orders to combat power of attorney misuse. Non-blood relation power of attorney documents will now face collector evaluation. This move aims to curb revenue loss and irregular practices at sub-registrar offices. Documents will be scrutinized for monetary consideration and property transfer provisions. Strict disciplinary action awaits sub-registrars who register GPAs involving monetary gains.

news.title: Delhi civic body's drone-based property survey pilot project to kick off soon
news.para: The Municipal Corporation of Delhi will pilot drone technology for property tax assessment. This initiative aims to identify unassessed properties and reduce revenue leakages. Advanced mapping technologies will be employed by four selected agencies for the survey. The project will help improve tax collection and urban planning efforts. Successful pilots may lead to a city-wide expansion of this new system.

news.title: Regularisation fee deters Delhi's unauthorised colony residents from legalising properties
news.para: The MCD's property regularisation drive has received only five applications. Many low-income residents cannot afford the high legalisation charges. Officials are discussing reducing these charges with the Delhi government. There is also no deadline, which reduces the urgency for residents. Awareness campaigns and architect support are being provided to encourage applications.

news.title: Dharavi redevelopment phase-I to complete in 18 months: Maharashtra deputy CM
news.para: Mumbai's Chief Minister announced significant urban renewal and housing projects.  The Dharavi Redevelopment Project aims for economic transformation and modern infrastructure.  About ten thousand houses will be completed in the first phase within eighteen months.  Cluster redevelopment and a new tunnel will improve city connectivity and growth.  The government targets constructing over seven lakh houses in the Mumbai Metropolitan Region by 2030.

news.title: Assam government aims to construct two lakh houses under PMAY-G in 100 days
news.para: Assam's government plans to finish two lakh houses under PMAY-Gramin soon.  This initiative aims to provide new homes to many families across the state.  Since 2016, over twenty-two lakh houses have already been built.  The minister assured a review of alleged geo-tagging irregularities.  Eligible house lists are being prepared as per scheme guidelines.

news.title: Citizens’ group urges pause on NAKSHA land verification in Cuncolim
news.para: Citizens for Democracy Goa wants the government to pause property verification.  They demand formal guidelines and legal basis for the ongoing exercise.  Thousands of residents face anxiety due to the short 30-day deadline.  Many long-term residents lack complete historical documentation for their properties.  The group warns of legal uncertainty and potential chaos if the process continues.

news.title: PG rent rises across Chennai as food costs and LPG prices push up monthly expenses
news.para: Paying guest accommodation rents have significantly increased across Chennai and Tambaram. Operators have raised prices by at least thirty-five percent due to higher commercial LPG costs. Separate food charges have also been introduced, impacting student budgets. Many tenants now face higher monthly expenses for their shared living spaces. This situation is causing considerable financial strain for young professionals and students.
